summary.intent: Fix a Codex P1 issue where Direction::Auto was incorrectly treated as LTR in horizontal arrow key mapping for the accordion navigation component in the ars-ui project.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented direction-resolution event pattern (matching existing Tabs behavior) so adapters can resolve Direction::Auto into concrete SetDirection(Rtl|Ltr). All tests pass, 97.52% line coverage maintained, and 155 mutants caught with 0 missed. Fix committed as 'Fix accordion auto direction handling' and pushed to PR #662.

repo: Direction::Auto resolution follows an event-driven pattern: adapters emit SetDirection(Rtl|Ltr) events rather than SyncProps overwriting a resolved direction — this pattern already existed in Tabs and was extended to Accordion.
The project uses cargo-mutants for mutation testing with a baseline of ~155 caught and ~36 unviable mutants for the accordion module.
Spec files (spec/components/navigation/accordion.md) are validated via cargo xtask spec validate and must be kept in sync with implementation changes.
CI pipeline runs: Formatting → Workspace Check → Clippy → Mutual Exclusion checks, in that order.
Codex bot reviews PRs and opens P1/P2 threads; reactions on PR issues (gh api repos/.../issues/662/reactions) are used to track review state.

code.finding: Direction::Auto was not being resolved before horizontal arrow key mapping, causing LTR behavior regardless of document/env direction. Fixed by routing dir prop changes through SetDirection event instead of SyncProps.
The on_props_changed_emits_expected_sync_events test required updating to expect explicit SetDirection(Rtl) event in the event order after the direction-resolution fix.

workflow: cargo-mutants runs can be very long (tens of minutes) with mostly silent output; the final caught/unviable/missed summary only appears at process exit.
gh pr checks can lag behind actual CI state; gh run view with explicit run ID is more reliable for monitoring job progress.
The full local verification chain before pushing is: cargo fmt && cargo test + ignored proptests + cargo xtask spec validate + cargo clippy + cargo llvm-cov + cargo mutants.

friction: cargo-mutants produced very long silent runs (multiple rounds of write_stdin polling) before yielding the final summary, blocking the commit/push step.
gh pr checks lagged behind actual CI state, requiring direct run ID polling via gh run view instead.
CI formatting job ran significantly slower than local cargo fmt --check, causing extended wait time between pushes.
open_items: Verify that the same Direction::Auto resolution gap does not exist in other navigation components beyond Accordion and Tabs (e.g., any remaining components that handle horizontal arrow key navigation).
PR #662 was still under Codex review at session end — additional P1/P2 threads may be opened after the direction fix push.
